events.title: Close-Up Trip Begins
events.summary: The group leaves Puerto Rico for the Close-Up trip to Washington, D.C., and New York.
events.details: At the beginning of the trip, the boys and girls still largely operate as separate social circles. Over the next several days, shared travel, meals, calls, and activities begin turning them into one unified friend group.

events.title: Dining-Hall Unification
events.summary: The boys choose to sit with the girls in a large dining hall, creating the first clear unification of the two sides.
events.details: The exact day is not locked, but the event occurred before the breakfast conversation involving Valentin and Kateryna. From this point forward, the group increasingly traveled, ate, and socialized together.

events.title: Valentin Questions Kateryna About Gage
events.summary: During breakfast, Valentin asks Kateryna whether she likes Gage.
events.details: The question creates a brief awkward moment and foreshadows Gage’s later confession near the end of the trip.

events.title: Original Mozarella Sticks Group Chat Created
events.summary: Jeidenne creates the first Mozarella Sticks WhatsApp chat while the Close-Up trip is still underway.
events.details: The chat is initially created as “Mozzorella Sticks.” Its icon and description are set within minutes, and Lucas corrects the name to “Mozzarella Sticks” at 7:36 PM. The early membership is still fluid: several peripheral classmates are included, while Kateryna cannot yet be added because she does not have a phone. The chat gives the newly merging group a shared space across separate hotel rooms.

events.title: Cheddar Becomes the Group’s Shared Mascot
events.summary: Alejandro’s Minecraft pufferfish plush is already being treated as a shared member of the group.
events.details: Cheddar had been purchased earlier during Close-Up. By May 14, members were asking for updates about him, calling him their boy, and saying goodnight to him alongside the rest of the group. His presence became one of the trip’s clearest symbols of the new shared identity.

events.title: Hotel-Room Chats Deepen the Group Bond
events.summary: The new chat becomes active every morning and late at night while members are separated across hotel rooms.
events.details: Members use the chat to locate one another, coordinate downstairs meetings, share media from the day, ask who is still awake, and exchange repeated morning and goodnight messages. Lucas and Gage watch horror videos together, the girls continue conversations from their room, and the group remains socially connected even after the day’s organized activities end.

events.title: Morning Gartic Phone Sessions
events.summary: The group repeatedly plays Gartic Phone and other browser games during the hotel mornings.
events.details: Gartic Phone links are shared on May 15 and multiple times early on May 16, followed by a Skribbl.io session. The games give the group another shared activity outside the official Close-Up schedule and generate several memories that continue being referenced after the trip.

events.title: Cheddar Is Restricted to the Hotel Room
events.summary: A teacher says Cheddar can no longer be carried around after Bash repeatedly throws him during lunch at an Italian restaurant.
events.details: During lunch at an Italian restaurant, Bash repeatedly throws Cheddar around until Cynthia takes the plush away. That night, Alejandro tells the chat that Cheddar must remain in the hotel room. The restriction turns the plush from a simple souvenir into a shared subject of concern for the group.

events.title: Statue of Liberty Group Photos
events.summary: Members coordinate a group picture in front of the Statue of Liberty and continue taking photos from the ferry.
events.details: Lydia calls for a group picture at 10:35 AM while Alejandro shares his location near the monument. The chat is used to gather people who have split toward the cafeteria or other areas, and Lucas later asks whether everyone should meet again on top of the boat. The exchange captures how the newly formed group was still learning to move as one unit.

events.dateLabel: During the New York portion of Close-Up
events.title: Observation-Tower Photos After Sunset
events.summary: The group takes a set of memorable photographs from the top of an unnamed New York observation tower after sunset.
events.details: The group reaches the tower by elevator and enters an observation area with glass walls, public telescopes, and wide views of New York. The location was not the Empire State Building, but its exact name and calendar time remain unknown. The photographs became some of the most recognizable images from the trip and were heavily shared after everyone returned home.

events.title: Dash Leaves Close-Up Early
events.summary: Dash leaves the trip before the rest of the group so he can return for his birthday.
events.details: Shortly before separating from the group, Dash tells everyone that he will miss them and says he will see them again in August. The following morning, members tell him that they already miss him while the chat celebrates his birthday. His early departure creates the first goodbye of the newly unified group before Close-Up officially ends on May 18.

events.title: Gage Confesses to Kateryna
events.summary: After the group watches The Wiz, Gage stops Kateryna in the hotel hallway and confesses his feelings.
events.details: During the late-afternoon performance, Gage switches seats with Sebastian Machicote so he can sit beside Kateryna. After the show ends and the group returns to the Marriott after sunset, he stops Kateryna as she walks toward her room. She rejects him with “You’re a great guy, but…”. A few hours later, they speak over FaceTime from their rooms and resolve the awkwardness.

events.title: The Return-Flight Salute
events.summary: As the group returns to Puerto Rico, members post a chain of salute emojis in the original chat.
events.details: Lucas begins the documented sequence at 3:43 PM, followed by Gage, Lydia, Shane, Alejandro, Valentin, Jeidenne, and Alexander. The exchange becomes a clear written record of the salute tradition at the end of the trip.

events.title: The Group Begins Recording Its Own Lore
events.summary: Within hours of returning home, members repeatedly expand the chat description with references to the trip and one another.
events.details: The group description is changed several times during the evening, with Dash noting that he added material and Lydia asking why she was being called out. Although the exported chat does not preserve the old description text, the edits show the group beginning to document its shared identity immediately after Close-Up.

events.title: Close-Up Ends
events.summary: The group returns to Puerto Rico with the new friendship intact and continues communicating immediately.
events.details: On the final morning, members coordinate packing and exchange messages before the flight. As the plane returns to Puerto Rico, the chat fills with a chain of salute emojis between 3:43 and 3:59 PM. Instead of returning to the earlier boys-versus-girls separation, the group remains active online and begins preserving the trip through photos, descriptions, and edits.

events.title: The Group Archives Close-Up
events.summary: Members exchange hundreds of photographs and create edits celebrating the trip immediately after returning home.
events.details: Alejandro uploads a large photo collection on May 19, followed by another extensive archive from Dash. Dash tells the group that they made the trip amazing. On May 20, Alejandro shares a trip edit that Lucas says represents the experience perfectly, and Dash begins producing another edit while changing the group icon to a trip image. This immediate preservation effort helps turn Close-Up into the group’s founding story.

events.title: First Post–Close-Up Group Hangout
events.summary: The group meets at the mall and Dave & Buster’s for its first major hangout after the trip.
events.details: The group is still somewhat divided during parts of the arcade visit, but everyone becomes noticeably closer by the end of the day.

events.title: The Original Chat Narrows Its Membership
events.summary: Several peripheral members are removed as the group’s active boundaries become clearer.
events.details: Dominique and two other early participants are removed from the original Close-Up chat. The change reflects the difference between people included during the trip’s early excitement and the smaller group that remained consistently active afterward.

events.title: Updated WhatsApp Group Created
events.summary: A newer, more current Mozarella Sticks WhatsApp group is created alongside the original Close-Up chat.
events.details: The June 14 chat becomes the more up-to-date group space, but it does not fully replace the May 14 chat. The original remains intermittently active through June 2026, meaning the two chats coexist for much of the group’s history.

events.title: “Crushing Too Hard” Playlist Incident
events.summary: A community Spotify playlist titled “Crushing too hard” appears on Lydia’s television during a hangout.
events.details: Lydia did not create the playlist; she searched for and opened a community-made playlist. Nobody confirmed that it was connected to Lucas, although the group found that interpretation easy to make because Lucas and Lydia were growing closer at the time.

events.title: Alejandro and Kateryna Admit Their Feelings
events.summary: Alejandro and Kateryna agree to an unofficial arrangement after admitting they like each other.
events.details: Because Kateryna is not allowed to date, they decide they are not officially together despite mutually acknowledging their feelings. The arrangement quickly becomes awkward and unstable.

events.title: Lucas and Lydia Begin an Unofficial Arrangement
events.summary: Lucas and Lydia begin a similar undefined relationship approximately one or two days after Alejandro and Kateryna.
events.details: The exact date is not confirmed. Like the other pairing, the relationship lacks a clear formal label and becomes increasingly awkward.

events.title: Both Summer Arrangements End
events.summary: Kateryna ends her arrangement with Alejandro, and Lydia ends hers with Lucas.
events.details: The endings occur close together. Alejandro and Lucas support each other afterward, while both former pairings enter eighth grade with lingering awkwardness.

events.title: September Reset
events.summary: A group hangout helps ease the awkwardness left by the summer relationships.
events.details: The group becomes more comfortable functioning together again. Around this period, Libby receives her own phone and becomes more active in group communication.

events.title: Libby Joins the Updated WhatsApp Group
events.summary: Libby is added to the newer Mozarella Sticks WhatsApp chat.
events.details: This supports the broader shift in which she becomes more active in group communication after receiving her own phone.

events.title: Lucas and Leah Date
events.summary: Lucas begins a relationship with Leah that lasts approximately two months.
events.details: Their first kiss occurs on October 25, around the middle-to-later portion of the relationship. The relationship ends around mid-November. The supporting December evidence is not treated as a separate timeline event.

events.title: Todd and Aedan Integrate
events.summary: Todd and Aedan become increasingly involved in the Mozarella Sticks’ social orbit.
events.details: Todd’s integration is reinforced by a smaller carnival outing with Alejandro and Kateryna. By January 2025, both Todd and Aedan are fully comfortable within the group.

events.title: Model UN Competition at Saint Johns
events.summary: Students participate in a two-day Model UN competition at Saint Johns.
events.details: The competition runs from November 8 through November 9. The available timeline does not yet identify the winning delegation, highest-placing group, or individual awards.

events.title: Libby and Zach Begin Dating
events.summary: Libby begins dating Zach, an athletic boy outside the core friend group.
events.details: The relationship is well received by the Mozarella Sticks and remains stable through the end of eighth grade and into ninth grade.

events.title: Grade-Wide Party Bus Adventure
events.summary: The grade spends the day together on a party bus and at several recreational stops.
events.details: The outing includes dancing on the bus, drawing in the beach sand, eating ice cream, and watching the sunset from a Ferris wheel. No major conflict occurs; it becomes a broad shared grade memory that includes the Mozarella Sticks.

events.title: A Stable Period
events.summary: The group enters a calmer period after the social awkwardness of early eighth grade.
events.details: Todd and Aedan are fully integrated. Gage is in a steady relationship outside the group, and the friend group’s internal dynamics are comparatively stable.

events.title: Chick-fil-A Photo Incident
events.summary: A photograph makes it look as though Kateryna is feeding Alejandro, leading to teasing.
events.details: In reality, Alejandro and Kateryna had intertwined their arms and were feeding themselves. The event is minor but shows that they were comfortable interacting playfully again.

events.title: Lucas and Lydia Begin Dating
events.summary: Lucas and Lydia officially become a couple on Valentine’s Day.
events.details: The relationship begins publicly without a separate timeline section about a secret reconnection. Alejandro also gives teddy bears to group members that day.

events.title: Model UN Competition at Baldwin
events.summary: Students participate in a two-day Model UN competition at Baldwin.
events.details: The competition runs from February 21 through February 22. The available timeline does not yet identify the winning delegation, highest-placing group, or individual awards.

events.title: Spirit Week Dance Era
events.summary: Practices and post-practice outings become a major bonding period for the group.
events.details: Temporary dance pairings and repeated rehearsals create some of the group’s strongest eighth-grade memories.

events.title: The Track Pantsing Prank
events.summary: Jeidenne and Bash pull a sudden prank on Alejandro during P.E., creating an awkward but funny shared memory.
events.details: While the group is walking around the track in partially separated clusters, Jeidenne and Bash call Lorena’s name so several girls turn around, then pants Alejandro in front of them. Alejandro immediately pulls his pants back up as the group reacts with surprise and laughter. The incident is remembered as chaotic humor rather than a lasting conflict.

events.title: Kamilo’s Bowling Birthday Party
events.summary: Most of the Mozarella Sticks gather at a bowling alley to celebrate Kamilo’s jokingly proclaimed 47th birthday.
events.details: Kamilo’s birthday party brings together a large portion of the group at a bowling alley filled with music and food. Everyone spends the event bowling, celebrating, and enjoying the gathering, with the occasion remembered as Kamilo’s 47th birthday.

events.title: Promposals
events.summary: Alejandro asks Lorena, Lucas asks Lydia, and Shane asks Kateryna to prom.
events.details: Lorena and Lydia accept their invitations. Kateryna rejects Shane, which hurts him. The exact order and dates of the three promposals remain unknown.

events.title: Gage’s Beach Moment
events.summary: Gage shares a romantic beach moment with his girlfriend shortly before prom.
events.details: The event becomes much more relevant around prom day, when the group is discussing everyone’s romantic moments and plans.

events.title: Eighth-Grade Prom
events.summary: The group attends prom, dances, takes photographs, and continues to an after-party into the next morning.
events.details: Alejandro brings Lorena white tulips and a sunflower with Lydia’s help. Libby has a romantic moment with Zach at the school bell tower. The after-party continues into the early hours of May 18.

events.title: Caguas Botanical Garden Field Trip
events.summary: The grade visits a botanical garden in Caguas to explore nature and learn about local cultural history.
events.details: Students walk through the grounds, observe plants and fish in the ponds, and learn from the garden’s cultural and historical exhibits. The trip becomes a relaxed shared school memory between prom and graduation.

events.title: Eighth-Grade Graduation
events.summary: The grade graduates, Student Council members give speeches, and the group begins saying goodbye to Gage.
events.details: The date is confirmed by the WhatsApp discussion surrounding the ceremony. Gage becomes emotional knowing he will soon leave Puerto Rico.

events.title: Lucas and Lydia’s First Proper Date
events.summary: The group stages a fake hangout so Lucas and Lydia can spend the day alone together.
events.details: Lucas tells everyone to act as though they are going to his house. Lydia believes it is a normal group gathering and tells her parents the group will be there. The others intentionally stay away, giving Lucas and Lydia their first full day together without friends present.

events.title: Gage’s Final Boys’ Sleepover
events.summary: Gage hosts one final sleepover with the boys before moving to Connecticut.
events.details: The sleepover follows graduation and serves as Gage’s last major boys-only gathering before leaving Puerto Rico. His exact moving date remains unknown.

events.title: Lucas Leaves for France
events.summary: Lucas begins his Summer 2025 trip to Europe.
events.details: The WhatsApp export captures Lucas discussing the plane’s movement and later traveling through Madrid toward Barcelona.

events.title: The France Incident
events.summary: While in France, Lucas holds hands with another girl who reminds him of Lydia.
events.details: Lucas later feels guilty about the interaction. The event becomes the central cause of the collapse of his relationship with Lydia.

events.title: Lucas Returns to Puerto Rico
events.summary: Lucas returns from France one day before confessing the incident to Lydia.
events.details: His return fixes the chronology of the confession and the later in-person flower apology.

events.title: Lucas Confesses and Lydia Ghosts Him
events.summary: Lucas tells Lydia what happened in France, and she immediately stops responding normally to him in private.
events.details: Lydia continues participating in the wider Mozarella Sticks chat and remains socially active with others. Her withdrawal is directed specifically toward Lucas, often through silence or extremely vague private messages.

events.title: The Core Four Forms
events.summary: Alejandro, Lucas, Kateryna, and Lorena begin spending significant time together as a four-person subgroup.
events.details: The subgroup forms during the tension following the France confession and becomes especially associated with movie outings and smaller summer plans.

events.title: Core Four Trampoline Park Outing
events.summary: The Core Four visits a trampoline park and then goes to the mall.
events.details: The outing becomes one of the clearest shared memories of the subgroup’s Summer 2025 period.

events.title: Lucas Attempts a Flower Apology
events.summary: Lucas and Jeidenne visit Lydia’s house with flowers, but Lydia does not open the door.
events.details: Lucas leaves the flowers at the front entrance for Lydia to collect. She continues ghosting him and responding vaguely afterward. The date is approximate rather than confirmed as June 26.

events.title: Lucas and Lydia Break Up
events.summary: Lydia officially ends the relationship while Lucas is playing REMATCH with friends.
events.details: Lucas shows the breakup message to his friends at 12:30 AM. A later-rendered video supports the REMATCH context, but its 12:42 filename timestamp is not the breakup time.

events.title: Josie and Alexander Begin Dating
events.summary: After nearly a year of gradual buildup, Josie and Alexander officially begin a relationship around the start of ninth grade.
events.details: Their relationship is remembered as one of the friend circle’s least controversial and most stable romantic developments. The exact date remains unknown, so the event is placed approximately in mid-August.

events.title: Samantha Rapidly Integrates
events.summary: Samantha joins the current WhatsApp group and quickly adapts to the Mozarella Sticks’ humor and conversations.
events.details: Her integration begins through the chat from August 23 onward. The August 29 gathering at Gage’s former Puerto Rico home becomes a major in-person bonding moment rather than her absolute first interaction.

events.title: Ninth-Grade Chat Expansion
events.summary: Several people are added to the current WhatsApp chat as ninth grade begins.
events.details: Samantha is added August 23, Marife August 26, Dash August 27, and Nicholas August 29. These dates represent chat membership, not necessarily the beginning of each friendship.

events.title: Gathering at Gage’s Former Puerto Rico Home
events.summary: Part of the group gathers at Gage’s former house while he is visiting Puerto Rico.
events.details: Gage stays at the same home where he lived before moving. Samantha’s growing closeness with the group is reinforced during the gathering.

events.title: Maria Becomes Closely Integrated
events.summary: Maria becomes especially close with the girls and increasingly present in the wider group’s daily life.
events.details: She regularly talks with the girls, sits with them at lunch, and spends time with the group. Her later addition to the WhatsApp chat does not mark the beginning of her friendship with everyone.

events.title: Welcome-Back Beach Party
events.summary: The grade gathers at the beach for a welcome-back party with music, water activities, and a sunset picnic.
events.details: The party includes a DJ, a Spuma foam party, surfing classes, and sunset picnics with s’mores. It becomes a broad grade-wide memory early in ninth grade. Alongside that, Gage came back.

events.title: Private Screenshots Spread
events.summary: Screenshots involving Jeidenne and Aedan begin circulating and indirectly affect Samantha.
events.details: Jeidenne had flirted with both Aedan and Samantha in different ways. He shared screenshots with selected people, including Jaime, who was most likely the point through which they spread to the wider group. Samantha and Aedan briefly distance themselves from Jeidenne. The conflict receives no clear apology or proper resolution.

events.title: Alejandro and Maria Decide Against Dating
events.summary: Alejandro and Maria discuss a possible relationship but agree not to begin one.
events.details: During the discussion, Alejandro says Maria is “not my type at all,” leaving a brief negative mark on her. Their feelings later change without this becoming a major dramatic reversal.

events.title: Halloween Split
events.summary: Part of the boys secretly leaves Dash’s house to meet the girls, leaving another group behind.
events.details: Bash, Lucas, Shane, Todd, and Jeidenne leave. Alejandro, Nicholas, Marco, Alexander, and Dash remain at Dash’s house and feel hurt after realizing what happened. Alejandro calls Maria and asks her to come over. Maria later speaks with him privately in Dash’s second living room while the others can see them through the windows.

events.title: Serafina Joins the Current Group Chat
events.summary: Serafina is added to the Mozarella Sticks WhatsApp group.
events.details: She is initially one of the newer members but plays a larger role later in ninth grade.

events.title: BRAVA Grade Party
events.summary: Student Council hosts a formal grade party at BRAVA.
events.details: Students are invited to dress nicely and dance at the luxurious club venue. No major conflict occurs; the event becomes another shared social memory for the Mozarella Sticks and the wider grade.

events.title: Dash Takes Music Production More Seriously
events.summary: Dash begins sharing original beats and songs more regularly with the boys.
events.details: He had already shown music during school hours before the dated chat evidence. By late November, the boys’ encouragement helps turn production into a serious hobby. His work later reaches the full Mozarella Sticks chat.

events.title: Alejandro and Maria Begin Dating
events.summary: After their feelings gradually change, Alejandro and Maria privately begin a relationship.
events.details: During a late-night conversation, they compare their standards for relationships and reflect on their past time together. Alejandro confesses, Maria reciprocates, and they agree to keep the relationship secret temporarily. Lucas suspects them almost immediately, while Libby figures it out around mid-December.

events.title: Spirit Week Mix Conflict Begins
events.summary: A dedicated dance chat is created, and several dancers object to the original no-theme production.
events.details: The organizers explain that suggestions had been requested in September and that Alejandro’s original mix was already prepared. Opponents argue that the grade needs a clear theme and that the dedicated planning chat was created too late. A compromise allows the original production to continue temporarily while another group creates an alternative.

events.title: First Spirit Week Practice
events.summary: The first documented dance practice takes place at Sebastian Machicote’s house using Alejandro’s original mix.
events.details: The practice establishes that the first production had reached a usable rehearsal stage before the replacement was selected.

events.title: Careers Mix Presented
events.summary: The alternative group presents a careers-and-occupations concept and opens a vote against the original mix.
events.details: The new concept is creatively stronger but remains an unfinished draft that exceeds the five-minute limit and requires improved transitions. The debate becomes personal, with insults toward the original mix and defensive sarcasm from Alejandro.

events.title: Mix Two Wins the Vote
events.summary: The careers production wins and replaces Alejandro’s original mix.
events.details: Alejandro stops developing the first production. The decision resolves the immediate vote but leads into delayed choreography, inconsistent attendance, and repeated rehearsal difficulties.

events.title: Libby Learns Zach Will Move
events.summary: Libby is informed that Zach is expected to move away during Summer 2026.
events.details: She is devastated. They agree that they will break up when he leaves, not because of anger or lost feelings, but because of the separation. The exact date when she receives the news remains unknown.

events.title: Exploration Week
events.summary: The school holds a three-day event built around students’ preferred classes and activities.
events.details: The event is confirmed, but the individual activities and major memories have not yet been fully collected.

events.title: Chili’s Gathering
events.summary: A sizable portion of the Mozarella Sticks meets at Chili’s.
events.details: The outing is a smaller ninth-grade social memory rather than a major turning point. Maria is also added to the current WhatsApp group on this date.

events.title: Alejandro and Aedan’s Joke Rivalry Begins
events.summary: Aedan confronts Alejandro over a joke video involving Samantha, beginning their pretend “beef.”
events.details: Nicholas jokes that they are going to start fighting. February 4 is the first explicit chat confirmation that Alejandro and Aedan call the rivalry “beefing.” The joke repeatedly ends and restarts over later minor disagreements.

events.title: Tuna de Segreles School Event
events.summary: A large portion of the social circle participates in a school event, with several members working assigned shifts.
events.details: Shift workers included Lucas, Jeidenne, Dash, Kamilo, Sebastian Machicote, Todd, Leo, Kateryna, Lorena Viñuela, and Lydia. Leo appears in the event but is not a Mozarella Sticks member.

events.title: Alejandro and Maria Reveal Their Relationship
events.summary: The couple publicly reveals that they have been dating since December 2.
events.details: Most of the girls are shocked, while many of the boys are unsurprised because they had already suspected it. Serafina and Dash are especially enthusiastic and supportive.

events.title: Lucas and Samantha Begin Growing Closer
events.summary: Lucas and Samantha begin talking more frequently, creating the earliest romantic speculation.
events.details: Only a small number of people notice or speculate at this stage.

events.title: Kateryna and Lorena Win Model UN
events.summary: Kateryna and Lorena Viñuela place first in a Model UN competition.
events.details: The competition runs from January 30 through January 31. After winning, they share a selfie captioned “Winner winner chicken dinner.”

events.title: Lydia Finds a Cat
events.summary: Lydia asks the group whether anyone wants to adopt a cat she found.
events.details: Marco expresses interest but never adopts it. The exchange remains a minor group-chat memory.

events.title: Lucas and Samantha Begin Dating
events.summary: Lucas asks Samantha to begin dating, and she accepts.
events.details: The relationship is initially kept highly private. Alejandro, Maria, and Aedan are among the small number of people who know.

events.title: Libby Gives Zach a Sword
events.summary: Libby gives Zach a sword for Valentine’s Day after hearing that swords are the male equivalent of flowers.
events.details: Zach posts it the next day with “Best gift ever.” Alejandro shares the post in the group chat, and the group congratulates Libby.

events.title: Spirit Week Dance
events.summary: The ninth-grade dance places second to last, dropping the grade from third to fifth overall.
events.details: Before the dance, ninth grade was ranked third overall, largely because it had won the skit competition. The careers-themed Mix Two production placed second to last among the competing grades. The result briefly reignited the mix controversy. Both sides generally agreed that the late replacement and lost rehearsal time hurt the grade’s chances, although it cannot be proven that Mix One would have placed better.

events.title: Lucas Visits Washington, D.C.
events.summary: Lucas travels to Washington, D.C., revisiting at least one location associated with the Close-Up trip.
events.details: He arrives on February 25 and returns to Puerto Rico on March 2, his birthday.

events.title: Battle of the Bands
events.summary: The school performs at a late-night Battle of the Bands hosted by another school.
events.details: Marco and Leo performed on stage, while Jeidenne, Kamilo, Josie, and Leah watched from the audience. The event is remembered positively for the bands’ performances, without a clear placement being recorded in the timeline. Leah and Leo are connected students, not Mozarella Sticks members.

events.title: Mysterious Notes Appear on Lucas’s Golf Cart
events.summary: Lucas finds two anonymous notes on his golf cart, leading Jeidenne to suspect a secret admirer.
events.details: The notes read “IM Always Watching” and “your hairline looks Good today (not balding yet).” The sender is eventually revealed to be Todd.

events.title: Serafina Shares a Possible Future Schedule
events.summary: Serafina leaks a potential school schedule for the following year, initially confusing and shocking the group.
events.details: As the conversation continues, many members become more interested in and positive about the possible changes. The schedule is treated as preliminary rather than officially confirmed.

events.title: Josie’s Themed Birthday Party
events.summary: Josie hosts a princess-and-character-themed birthday party attended by a large portion of the expanded group.
events.details: Picture timestamps confirm the party occurred on March 7. Attendees included Marife, Samantha, Josie, Kateryna, Libby, Lorena Viñuela, Serafina, Lydia, Gage, Lucas, Alejandro, Todd, Nicholas, Sienna, and Jeidenne, with Dash arriving later. The party reflects Josie’s increased prominence and brings together established and newer members.

events.title: Lucas and Samantha Reveal Their Relationship
events.summary: Lucas and Samantha publicly reveal their relationship same day as Josie's birthday party.
events.details: They had been dating privately since Valentine’s Day. The reveal ends the relationship’s highly limited secrecy.

events.title: Sienna Joins the Current Group Chat
events.summary: Sienna is added to the current Mozarella Sticks chat after speaking with Alejandro about joining.
events.details: Her addition follows her attendance at Josie’s birthday party the previous day and marks her formal entry into the group’s main communication space.

events.title: Planning Future Classes
events.summary: Students begin visiting Laura’s office to discuss course selections for the next school year.
events.details: Many members discussed ambitious schedules containing several Advanced Placement classes. The exact students, meetings, and individual course selections remain incomplete.

events.title: Hallway Birthday Performance for Maria
events.summary: The boys gather with instruments and sing “Happy Birthday” to Maria in the school hallway.
events.details: A group of boys brought instruments and performed “Happy Birthday” for Maria in the school hallway. The exact performers have not yet been identified.

events.title: Alejandro Moves Homes
events.summary: Alejandro moves to a different home while remaining in the same town.
events.details: The move changes his home base without removing him from the group’s local social environment.

events.title: Samantha’s Phone Disappears and Returns
events.summary: Samantha’s phone appears to be stolen, causing panic as Find My shows it moving around Puerto Rico.
events.details: The phone is mysteriously returned later the same day. The group never learns who took it or how it was returned.

events.title: School Production of Mary Poppins
events.summary: The school presents Mary Poppins with several Mozarella Sticks members and relatives performing on stage.
events.details: Kateryna, Serafina, Lorena Viñuela, and Lydia perform, alongside Alejandro’s sister and Lorena’s sister.

events.title: The Screenshot Story Spreads Beyond the Group
events.summary: The private situation has become widely known throughout the grade.
events.details: Aedan and Jeidenne have resumed interacting, but direct jokes about the incident can still cause discomfort. The friendships recover socially without a clear apology or formal resolution.

events.title: Project Hail Mary Movie Reunion
events.summary: About half of the group reunites at the movies to watch Project Hail Mary.
events.details: Attendees include Sebastian Machicote, Lucas, Kateryna, Lydia, Serafina, Nicholas, Jeidenne, Alejandro, Todd, Maria, Samantha, Kamilo, and Leah. Leah attends as a connected friend but is not a Mozarella Sticks member.

events.title: Tyler, The Creator Concert
events.summary: Several members attend a Tyler, The Creator concert.
events.details: Nicholas, Kateryna, Alejandro, and Dash attend together, while Marife and Serafina attend separately.

events.title: School-Hosted Model UN Competition
events.summary: The school hosts a three-day Model UN competition.
events.details: The competition runs from April 10 through April 12. The available timeline does not yet identify the winning delegation, highest-placing group, or individual awards.

events.title: Alejandro Creates a Tomodachi Life Island
events.summary: Alejandro starts a Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream island featuring versions of most group members.
events.details: He rarely presents the island publicly, but members occasionally ask how their Miis are doing. It becomes a small digital representation of the friend group.

events.title: Vaqueros vs. Cangrejeros Feud Begins
events.summary: Jeidenne and Sebastian begin a playful rivalry centered on the Vaqueros and Cangrejeros.
events.details: The feud begins specifically between Jeidenne, supporting the Vaqueros, and Sebastian, supporting the Cangrejeros. Its later development can be expanded as more events are documented.

events.title: Student Council Campaigns and Election
events.summary: Several Mozarella Sticks members run, but Lucas is the only one who wins.
events.details: Campaigns are heavily discussed on April 22, and voting occurs April 23. Lucas becomes Vice President. Every other Mozarella Sticks candidate loses, while the remaining positions go to students outside the current group chat. The Spirit Week conflict affected the social atmosphere around the election, but the exact influence remains unclear.

events.title: Final Assignment and Wendy’s in the Physics Classroom
events.summary: A sizable portion of the group stays in the physics classroom to finish a final assignment and spend lunch together.
events.details: After working, the group talks, laughs, and shares Wendy’s for lunch. Dash arranges the food order, turning an ordinary final-assignment session into a relaxed shared memory.

events.title: Zach Moves Away
events.summary: Zach leaves Puerto Rico on June 20.
events.details: Libby had known for months that the move was coming. The exact timing and circumstances of their planned breakup have not yet been confirmed.
